description: In an apartment kitchen, Joey Sr., wearing a brown coat, speaks to Gloria, who wears a leopard-print top, while Joey, in a black sweater, stands slightly behind them. Joey Sr. gestures with both hands and says, "Train. No, honey, I don't want you taking that thing this." Gloria asks, "Where am I going to stay? Here."
Joey steps forward, holding his hands up, and says, "Whoa."
Joey Sr. turns back toward Gloria.
Joey Sr. gestures with one hand and says, "We'll go to a hotel."
Gloria turns and begins to walk toward the living area.
Gloria says, "Go to a hotel," as she walks past Joey.
Joey steps in front of Gloria, blocking her path.
Joey says, "No, you won't." Joey Sr. replies, "No, we won't."
Joey points at Joey Sr. and says, "If you go to a hotel, you'll be doing stuff. I want you right here where I can keep"
Joey continues, "...an eye on you." Joey Sr. puts his hands on his hips.
Joey Sr. points a finger at Joey and asks, "You're going to keep an eye on us?" Joey points his finger back and says, "That's right, Mr."
Joey wags his finger at Joey Sr., saying, "And I don't care how old you are. As long as you're under my roof, you're going to live by my rule."
Joey Sr. lowers his hands and looks at Joey as audience laughter plays.
Joey points at Joey Sr. again and says, "And that means no sleeping with your girlfriend."
Joey Sr. raises his eyebrows and looks toward Gloria.
Gloria looks at Joey Sr. and says, "Wow, he's strict."
Joey continues to look toward Joey Sr.
Joey points to the side, saying, "Now, dad, you'll be in my room. Ronnie, you can stay in Chandler's room."
Chandler walks out of a doorway into the living room as Joey adds, "And Chandler will be out"
Joey gestures toward the sofa and finishes, "...here with me bunking up on the hide-a-bed."
Chandler raises his eyebrows and says, "OOH. Bunking up? If you smell s'mores, don't be alarmed," giving a thumbs-up.
Gloria touches Joey's shoulder, saying, "Thanks. You're a good kid." She waves for Joey Sr. to follow and says, "Come on, I'll show you to my room," walking past Chandler.
Chandler watches Gloria walk away as Joey Sr. begins to follow her.
Chandler turns around and says, "That sounds so weird when it's not followed by, no, thanks, it's late." Audience laughter plays.
Chandler walks toward his room. Joey grabs Joey Sr. by the arm as he passes by.
Joey speaks in a lower volume, holding Joey Sr.'s arm, "Okay, now this is just for tonight. Starting tomorrow, you've got to make a change. Six years is long enough." Joey Sr. asks, "What kind"
Joey Sr. finishes, "...of change?" Joey gestures and says, "Either you break it off with Ronnie" Joey Sr. interrupts, "I can't do that."
Joey points his finger, "Then you got to come clean with Ma. This is not right." Joey Sr. waves his hands, "Yeah,"
Joey Sr. says, "...but this" before Joey points and firmly interrupts, "I don't want to hear it."
Joey points toward the hallway and says, "Now, go to my room."
Joey Sr. walks down the hallway, glancing back. Joey stands with his hands at his sides.
The scene changes to the exterior of a brick apartment building at night.
The setting changes to a dark room where Joey and Chandler lie side-by-side in a pull-out bed under patterned blankets. Joey shifts his legs around beneath the covers.
Joey turns his head toward Chandler and says, "Hey, Kiki."
Chandler turns his head to look at Joey.
Chandler asks, "What are you doing?"
Joey pulls his arms inside his t-shirt beneath the blanket and says, "Just trying to get comfortable. Can't sleep in my underwear."
Chandler quickly replies, "Well, you're gonna"
Joey shuffles beneath the blankets, pulling off his pants and tossing them to the floor beside the bed. Chandler looks away.
Joey settles his head back onto his pillow and looks upward.
Joey continues to look at the ceiling and says, "about how I'm always seeing girls on top of girls."
Chandler turns his head back to look at Joey.
Chandler asks, "Are they end to end or tall, like pancakes?"
Joey turns his head to look at Chandler.
Joey looks back up and says, "You know what I mean about how I'm always going out with all these women. Because I always figured when the right one comes along I'll be"
Joey gestures slightly with one hand beneath the blanket, "...able to be a stand up guy and go the distance. You know, I'm looking at my dad thinking"
Chandler turns his head toward Joey and interrupts, "hey, you're not him. You're"
Chandler finishes, "...you."
Chandler continues, "When they were all over you to go into your father's pipe fitting business did you cave?"
Joey looks at Chandler, remaining still.
Joey replies softly, "No." Chandler says, "No. You decided to go into the out of work actor business."
Joey keeps his gaze on Chandler.
Chandler continues, "Now, that wasn't easy, but you did it. And I'd like to believe that when the right woman comes along you will have the courage"
Chandler finishes, "...and the guts to say no, thanks, I'm married." Joey looks at him as audience laughter plays.
Joey smiles slightly and looks up at the ceiling as the laughter continues.
Joey turns his head back toward Chandler.
Joey asks in a softer volume, "Really think so?" Chandler replies, "Yeah, I really do." Joey rolls onto his side, moving closer to Chandler, and says, "Thanks, Chandler."
Chandler shoves Joey's shoulder away and says, "Get off."
Joey rolls onto his back, pulling the blanket up as audience laughter plays. They both settle onto their pillows.
The scene changes to a wide exterior view of a city skyline across the water at dawn.
Inside an apartment kitchen, Monica opens the front door. Ronni stands in the hallway holding a folded towel.
Ronni says, "Hi."
Monica smiles and leans against the doorframe.
Monica asks, "May I help you?" Ronni replies, "Yeah, Joey said I could use your shower since Chandler's in ours."
Monica asks, "Okay. Who are you?"
Ronni replies, "Oh, I'm Ronnie. Ronnie Rapalano, the mistress."
Monica invites her in with a hand gesture. Rachel, wearing denim overalls, walks into the kitchen.
Monica says, "Come on in." Ronni steps inside, saying, "Oh, thanks." Rachel says, "Hi, I'm Rachel."
Ronni says, "Hi." Rachel points toward the hallway and says, "Bathroom's up there." Ronni says, "Great." Rachel steps closer and says, "Hey, listen, Ronnie."
Rachel asks, "How long would you say Chandler's been in the shower?"
Ronni replies, "Oh, like five minutes."
Rachel smiles.
Rachel says, "Perfect."
Rachel walks past them and opens the front door.
Rachel steps into the hallway and says, "Fasten your seatbelts."
Rachel adds, "It's pee pee time."
The scene changes to Joey's apartment kitchen. Joey Sr. stands at the island reading a newspaper. Rachel enters through the front door.
Rachel walks toward the kitchen area.
Rachel says, "Hey, Mr."
Rachel finishes, "Trib." Joey Sr. looks up and says, "Hey. Good morning, dear."
Rachel walks past him toward the bathroom door.
Rachel places her hands flat on the closed bathroom door and says, "Chandler Bing, it's time to see your thing."
Rachel turns the doorknob, pushes the door open, and walks into the bathroom.
Rachel stands in front of the closed shower curtain and pulls it open.
Rachel screams. Joey, wearing a towel around his waist, screams and runs out of the bathroom past Rachel. Joey Sr. looks up. Audience laughter plays.
Joey turns and shouts, "What's the matter with you?"
Rachel steps out of the bathroom and points at Joey. Audience laughter continues.
Rachel points firmly at Joey and shouts, "You were supposed to be in there so I could see your thing."
Chandler steps out of his bedroom wearing a dark bathrobe, adjusting his sleeves. Audience laughter plays.
Chandler points toward the bathroom and says, "Sorry. My thing was in there with"
Chandler points at his own chest. Rachel stares at him with her mouth slightly open. The scene changes to a daytime exterior view of a busy city street.
Inside the Central Perk coffee shop, Monica, Chandler, and Ross sit at the sofas. Phoebe walks toward them, removing her coat.
Phoebe asks, "you. How's it going?"
Multiple voices reply, "Good." Phoebe says, "Oh, Roger's having a dinner thing and he wanted me to invite you guys," as she pulls off her scarf.
Ross closes his magazine and looks away. Monica and Chandler exchange a glance. Silence is held.
Phoebe asks, "So, what's going?"
Monica stammers and gestures with her hands. Chandler raises his mug to his mouth.
Ross raises his hand and speaks unintelligibly.
Monica says, "I don't know. There's something about" while gesturing.
Chandler finishes, "basically, we just feel that he's we hate that guy."
Rachel, standing behind the sofa, winces and speaks unintelligibly.
Ross says, "Sorry," followed by "Thieves. We're sorry" from the group. Phoebe replies, "Okay." Audience laughter plays.
Phoebe asks, "Don't you think maybe, though, that's just that he's so perceptive that it freaked you out?"
Ross shakes his head and replies, "No we"
Rachel speaks briefly. The scene changes to Joey's apartment, where Joey pushes the frame of a sofa bed inward.
Joey folds the sofa bed into a couch.
Joey drops the seat cushions onto the sofa. He turns toward the front door as a knock sounds.
Joey walks to the door, opens it, and finds Gloria standing in the hallway holding a paper bag.
Joey says, "doing here."
Gloria hands the bag to Joey, saying, "I came to give you this." She slaps his head and adds, "And this. Oh, big ring." Joey rubs his head.
Gloria asks, "Why did you have to fill your father's"
Gloria continues, "head with all that garbage about making things right?"
Gloria says, "Things were fine the way they were. There's chicken in"
Gloria points at the bag Joey holds, saying, "there. Put it away."
Joey carries the bag toward the kitchen counter.
Gloria says, "For God's sake, Joey. Really." Joey sets the bag on the counter.
Joey turns back to Gloria, spreading his hands.
Joey asks, "Hold on. You knew?" Gloria spreads her arms and replies, "Of course I knew."
Gloria asks, "What do you think?"
Gloria says, "Your father is no James Bond. You should have heard some of his cover stories." Audience laughter plays.
Gloria mimics Joey Sr. with hand gestures, saying, "I'm sleeping over. At my accountant. I mean, what is that? Please."
Joey steps closer and asks, "So then how could you? I mean, how could? Do"
Gloria holds up her hands and asks, "you remember how your father used to be?"
Gloria claps her hands together once.
Gloria gestures as she says, "Always yelling, always yelling. Nothing made him happy. Nothing made him happy."
Gloria continues, "Nothing. Not that wood shop, not those"
Gloria finishes, "stupid little ships in the bottle."
Gloria drops her hands and says, "Nothing. Now he's happy. I mean, it's nice. He has a hobby." Audience laughter plays.
Joey stares at Gloria, his mouth slightly open and his hands raised with palms facing each other.
Joey says, "Ma, I don't mean to be disrespectful, but what the hell are you talking about?"
Joey keeps his hands raised, staring at Gloria.

In an apartment kitchen, Joey stands near a counter, his head bowed, while Ronni stands facing him. Although no dialogue is audible, an uncertain off-screen speaker is subtitled saying, "What about you? Me?"
Joey keeps his head bowed. An uncertain off-screen speaker is subtitled saying, "I'm fine," though no dialogue is audible.
Ronni gestures with her hand and says, "Look, honey, in an ideal world there'd be no her and your father would look like Sting." Joey raises his head and looks at her.
Joey continues to look at Ronni. Audience laughter is audible.
Ronni gestures with both hands as she says, "And I'll tell you something else. Ever since that poodle stuffer came along he's been so"
Ronni continues gesturing, "ashamed of himself that he's been more attentive. He's been more loving. It's like every day is our"
Ronni lowers her hands and finishes, "anniversary."
Joey says, "I'm happy for you." Ronni interrupts, turning slightly away and raising her hands, "Well, don't think, because now everything's screwed up." Joey's eyebrows raise slightly.
Ronni turns her back to Joey and takes a few steps.
Ronni turns back to face Joey, gesturing with her hands, and says, "I just want it the way it was." Joey watches her.
Joey looks down, his hands resting on the counter.
Joey says, "I'm sorry," raising his hands briefly from the counter.
Joey lowers his hands back to the counter.
Joey says, "I just did what I thought you'd want."
Ronni approaches Joey, reaching her hands toward his face.
Ronni touches Joey's face and says, "I know you did, cookie."
Ronni strokes his cheeks with both hands, saying, "Oh, I know you did," as Joey closes his eyes briefly.
Ronni lowers her hands to hold Joey's right hand in both of hers.
Holding his hand, Ronni asks, "So tell me,"
Ronni tilts her head slightly.
Ronni asks, "did you see her?"
Joey nods his head slightly.
Joey says, "Yeah."
Joey maintains eye contact with Ronni.
Joey says, "You're ten times prettier than she is." Ronni smiles, gently pats his cheek, and says, "That's sweet."
Audience laughter plays as Ronni continues to smile at Joey.
Ronni asks, "Could I take her?"
Joey raises the hand that Ronni is holding.
Joey points to the ring on her hand and says, "With this ring, no contest." Both smile.
Joey and Ronni hold hands, laughing together. Audience laughter is audible.
The setting changes to the exterior of Central Perk coffee shop, where a window displays the neon sign and patrons sitting inside.
Inside the coffee shop, Phoebe sits on a couch, holding a yellow mug. Roger approaches from behind the couch, bends over, and kisses her neck.
Phoebe startles, spilling some of her drink, and pushes Roger's face away, saying, "...ever." Roger stands upright and says, "I'm sorry."
Phoebe gestures emphatically with both hands. Roger stands behind the couch, looking down at her.
Phoebe says, "I have a thing. Which means you can't ever do that," continuing to gesture.
Roger walks around the side of the couch and sits down next to Phoebe. Phoebe sets her mug down on the table.
Roger asks, "What's wrong, sweetie?" Phoebe waves her hands outward and says, "Nothing."
Roger places his hand on Phoebe's leg and leans toward her.
Roger says, "What's wrong? Come," gently pulling her downward.
Phoebe says, "Okay," and lies down on the couch with her head resting on his lap.
Roger gently strokes Phoebe's hair as she rests her hands on her stomach.
Phoebe looks up at Roger and says, "I mean, it's nothing. I'm fine."
Roger continues to stroke her hair.
Phoebe says, "It's my friends. They have a liking problem with you in that they don't." Roger maintains a neutral expression, stroking her hair.
Roger stops stroking her hair, his mouth falls open slightly, and he points to his chest.
Roger asks, "They don't?"
Phoebe begins to sit up.
Phoebe sits fully upright, turning to face Roger, and says, "But they don't see all the wonderfulness that I see."
Phoebe continues looking at Roger.
Phoebe gestures with her hands, saying, "They don't see all the good stuff and all the sweet know they think you're a little"
Roger looks at her.
Roger asks, "what?"
Roger maintains his gaze on Phoebe.
Phoebe says, "Intense and creepy."
Roger's mouth opens wide. Audience laughter plays.
Phoebe points to herself, saying, "Oh, but I don't." Roger points to himself and says, "me, Phoebe."
Roger shakes his head and looks away from Phoebe.
Roger says, "Well, I'm not at all surprised they feel that way." Phoebe smiles, leaning toward him, and says, "You're not? See, that's why you're so great."
Roger says, "Actually, it's quite typical behavior when you have this kind of dysfunctional group dynamic." He clasps his hands in his lap.
Roger gestures continuously with both hands as he says, "This kind of codependent, emotionally stunted sitting in your stupid coffee house with"
Roger gestures toward the yellow mug on the table and says, "your stupid big cups which, I'm sorry, might as well have nipples on them." Audience laughter plays.
Phoebe's smile fades as she stares at Roger.
Roger clasps his hands to his chest, raising his pitch, and says, "And you're all like, oh, define me."
Roger leans slightly forward, continuing in a high pitch, "Define me."
Roger maintains his posture and expression.
Roger clenches his fists near his chest and says, "Love me. I need love."
Roger stares straight ahead. Phoebe remains silent.
In a living room, Rachel, Monica, and Ross sit on a sofa holding plates of pizza. Joey stands nearby.
Monica asks Joey, "So you talked to your dad, huh?"
Joey replies, "Yeah. He's gonna keep cheating on my mom like she wanted. My mom's gonna keep"
Joey continues, "pretending she doesn't know even though she does."
Joey gestures with his hands and adds, "And my little sister Tina can't see her husband anymore because he got a restraining order"
Joey stands in place, holding a napkin.
Joey says, "which has nothing to do with anything."
Joey gestures slightly and adds, "Except that I found out today."
The seated group continues eating. Chandler sits in a chair near Joey.
Chandler holds a slice of pizza and says, "Things sure have changed here on Walton's Mountain." Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel and Monica continue eating.
Ross on the sofa asks, "So, Joe, are you okay?"
Joey looks downward and says, "Yeah. I guess it's know they're parents."
Joey wipes his fingers and adds, "After a certain point, you gotta let go."
Joey remains standing.
Joey says, "Even if you know better, you got to let them make their own mistakes."
Rachel gestures with one hand and says, "And just think, in a couple of years we get to turn into"
Rachel finishes, "them."
Chandler looks toward Joey.
Chandler says, "If I turn into my parents, I'll either be an alcoholic blonde chasing after 20 year old boys or"
Chandler briefly pauses.
Chandler continues, "I'll end up like my mom." Audience laughter is heard.
Phoebe opens the front door and walks into the apartment wearing a patterned coat.
Phoebe says, "Hey." Several voices in the room murmur greetings.
Phoebe walks toward the center of the room.
Rachel asks, "How's it going?"
Phoebe stops near a chair.
Phoebe says, "Okay."
Phoebe adds, "Except I broke up with Roger."
The group groans in unison.
Joey nods slightly and says, "Right." Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe sits down on the arm of a chair.
Rachel asks, "What happened?"
Phoebe shrugs and says, "I don't know."
She continues, "I mean, he's a good person and he can be really sweet."
Phoebe continues, "And in some ways, I think he is so right for me."
She adds, "It's just I hate that guy." Audience laughter is audible.
The group continues sitting in the living room as audience laughter subsides.
The setting changes to a different apartment where Chandler sits at a table reading. Joey opens the front door and steps inside.
Chandler looks toward him and says, "Hey, Joey."
Joey closes the door, rubs his hands together, and says, "What's going on? Clear the tracks for the booby payback express."
Joey walks toward a closed door in the hallway and says, "Next stop, Rachel Green."
Joey grabs the doorknob of the closed door.
Joey throws the door open, and a woman's scream is heard. Joey steps back with his eyes widened.
Monica, wrapped in a towel and wearing a shower cap, steps out from the bathroom and yells, "Joey, what the hell were you doing?"
Joey looks downward and says, "Sorry, wrong boobies." Audience laughter is heard.
Joey quickly walks away.
The setting changes. Monica, fully dressed, opens the front door of another apartment and enters.
Monica walks through the kitchen toward a closed bathroom door, keeping her back to the camera.
Monica stands in the bathroom and places her hand on the closed shower curtain.
Monica pulls back the shower curtain, revealing Joey Sr. taking a shower. Monica says, "Hello, Joey."
Joey Sr. turns toward her, his eyes widened.
Joey Sr. covers his chest and says, "Hello, dear." Audience laughter is audible.
Monica covers her mouth with her hand.
The screen turns black.
The screen remains black.
